title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for an Indian feast at home!
longTailKeywords: authentic Indian butter chicken murgh makhani
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Classic Butter Chicken
Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic butter chicken (murgh makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and cream, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, an authentic Murgh Makhani recipe.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian curry known for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and succulent marinated chicken pieces. This classic recipe delivers an authentic taste experience, balancing a symphony of mild spices with a hint of sweetness and tang. It is a quintessential comfort food, perfect for family dinners or entertaining guests.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch cubes
Canned crushed tomatoes or tomato puree
Cashews, soaked in warm water for 15 mins

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to ensure all chicken pieces are coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for deeper flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp of ghee or butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Base for the Sauce
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tbsp of ghee or butter. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), cooking for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes or tomato puree to the pan, along with Kashmiri red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and garam masala. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the mixture. Add the soaked cashews and water or chicken broth, then bring to a simmer. Let it cook for another 5 minutes. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. Strain the sauce back into the pan for an extra silky texture.

instructions.title: Finish the Makhani Sauce
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over low heat. Stir in the sugar and salt to taste. Add the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer gently for 2-3 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the creamy makhani sauce. Stir gently to coat the chicken evenly. Let it simmer for another 5 minutes to ensure the chicken is heated through and absorbs the sauce's flavors.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with warm naan bread, roti, or steamed basmati rice. Enjoy your homemade classic Butter Chicken!

tips: For a richer, deeper flavor, consider smoking your marinated chicken pieces for a few minutes before cooking, or use a small piece of charcoal in a bowl with ghee to infuse smoky aroma (dhungar method).
Kashmiri red chili powder provides vibrant color without excessive heat. If unavailable, use paprika for color and a touch of cayenne for heat, adjusted to your preference.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.
article:
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a name that evokes images of rich, creamy, and subtly spiced comfort food. This iconic Indian dish has captivated palates worldwide, earning its place as a staple on restaurant menus and in home kitchens alike. Renowned for its tender chicken pieces luxuriating in a velvety tomato-based gravy, infused with butter and cream, it’s a culinary masterpiece that truly stands the test of time.

Originating in Delhi shortly after India's partition, Butter Chicken was born out of a desire to create something delicious from leftover tandoori chicken. The inventive chefs at Moti Mahal restaurant developed a rich, buttery tomato gravy to rehydrate and flavor the dry chicken, and thus, a legend was born. It’s more than just a dish; it's a culinary experience, and mastering the classic Butter Chicken Recipe is a truly rewarding endeavor. If you're ready to dive into one of India's most beloved creations, follow along for a comprehensive guide to the ultimate Butter Chicken Recipe.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, directly translates to "chicken with butter." It’s characterized by succulent pieces of chicken, traditionally marinated in yogurt and spices, then cooked and simmered in a rich, mild, and slightly sweet tomato-cream sauce. The hallmark of Murgh Makhani is its smooth, luscious gravy, which gets its depth from puréed tomatoes, aromatic ginger, garlic, and a blend of traditional Indian spices. The generous use of butter (makhan) and cream gives it its signature richness and silky texture, making it undeniably indulgent.

While often compared to Chicken Tikka Masala, Butter Chicken typically boasts a milder, sweeter, and tangier flavor profile, with less emphasis on intense heat and more on the creamy, buttery notes. It's a dish that embodies warmth, comfort, and the incredible complexity that Indian cuisine is celebrated for, making it a universal favorite among spice lovers and those new to Indian flavors alike.

## Ingredients You Need for Classic Butter Chicken

To embark on your journey to create the perfect classic Butter Chicken Recipe, gathering the right ingredients is key.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain full-fat yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Gravy:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter, plus more for garnish
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes, puréed
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to your spice preference)
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/4 cup heavy cream, plus more for garnish
*   1-2 teaspoons sugar or honey (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream or a pat of butter

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Butter Chicken

This classic Butter Chicken Recipe, also known as Murgh Makhani, promises a delightful journey for your taste buds.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, for maximum flavor and tenderness.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, to avoid overcrowding the pan. Cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, reduce heat to medium. Add 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Build the Gravy:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or puréed fresh tomatoes) and the 1/2 cup of water or chicken broth. Bring to a simmer. Add the turmeric powder, ground coriander, cumin powder, and red chili powder. Mix well and cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate from the edges.
5.  **Blend for Smoothness:** Remove the pan from heat and let the gravy cool slightly. Carefully transfer the gravy mixture to a blender or use an immersion blender to purée until completely smooth. For an extra silky, restaurant-quality texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on any solids. Discard the solids.
6.  **Finish the Gravy:** Return the smooth gravy to medium-low heat. Stir in the garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, sugar or honey, and salt to taste. Simmer for 5 minutes.
7.  **Combine and Serve:** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir well to coat the chicken. Pour in the 1/4 cup of heavy cream and gently stir until combined. Let it simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich sauce. Do not boil after adding cream.
8.  **Garnish and Enjoy:** Ladle the Butter Chicken into serving bowls. Garnish with a swirl of extra cream, a small pat of butter, and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Following these pro tips will ensure your homemade Butter Chicken Recipe rivals your favorite restaurant's.

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder:** For that characteristic vibrant red-orange color without excessive heat, use Kashmiri chili powder. It's milder than regular chili powder but delivers beautiful color.
*   **Don't Skip Kasoori Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken flavor. Lightly toast them in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding them to enhance their aroma.
*   **Smooth Gravy:** Blending and then straining the gravy is crucial for achieving that silky-smooth, restaurant-style texture. It takes a little extra effort but is well worth it.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste and adjust. The perfect Butter Chicken strikes a balance between sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy. Add more sugar/honey for sweetness, a squeeze of lemon juice for tang, or a pinch more chili powder for heat.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Use good quality butter and fresh, high-fat cream for the richest flavor and texture.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Once you've perfected your Butter Chicken Recipe, the next step is to serve it in a way that truly complements its rich flavors. Murgh Makhani is incredibly versatile and pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice dishes.

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, fluffy naan, especially garlic naan or butter naan, is the classic choice for scooping up every last drop of that delicious gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** A simple bowl of perfectly cooked basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Roti or Chapati:** For a lighter option, whole wheat roti or chapati makes an excellent accompaniment.
*   **Side Salad:** A refreshing kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon juice) can cut through the richness.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based raita (cucumber or mint) is also a great palate cleanser.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Butter Chicken Recipe is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary preferences or to explore new flavor dimensions.

*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute some of the cream with Greek yogurt (stirred in at the very end to prevent curdling). You can also bake or grill the chicken instead of pan-frying it.
*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** This recipe is easily adaptable for vegetarians. Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cheese) for Paneer Makhani, firm tofu for Tofu Makhani, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower, potatoes, and peas for a delightful Vegetable Makhani.
*   **Spice Level:** To increase the heat, add a finely chopped green chili along with the ginger-garlic paste, or increase the amount of red chili powder. For a milder version, reduce the chili powder or use paprika for color.
*   **Dairy-Free:** For a dairy-free option, use coconut milk or cashew cream instead of heavy cream, and a plant-based butter alternative. Note that this will slightly alter the flavor profile, giving it a subtle coconut undertone.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic smoky flavor reminiscent of tandoor-cooked chicken, you can briefly smoke the cooked chicken using a charcoal piece (dhungar method) before adding it to the gravy.

Butter Chicken is a testament to the incredible depth and deliciousness of Indian cuisine. Its creamy texture, balanced flavors, and tender chicken make it an irresistible dish for any occasion. So gather your ingredients and embark on the delicious adventure of preparing this iconic Butter Chicken Recipe today. Enjoy the process, savor the aroma, and delight in the rich, comforting flavors you've created right in your own kitchen!
